I caught up with Matt Klein, one of the famed developers behind the Envoy Proxy, and had a chat about AI and why the hype machine has gone too far. I think the industry is putting machines over people and the tradeoff leans too far in the rent seeker's favor.

one of the weirdest things I've witnessed in my career is Michael Burry becoming an AI booster that also pretends to care about the economics. He actively (and nonsensically - Claude Cowork doesn't really work!) boosts Anthropic again and again and refuses to cover the actual economics side.

recent_posts.text:
Koordinator saves money. OpenFeature saves migration pain. Both are CNCF projects solving problems nobody else bothered to standardize.

Full deep dive:
podostack.com/p/dapr-karg... 🛠️

Now - OpenFeature. Different problem, same pattern: vendor lock-in.

Every feature flag tool has its own SDK. LaunchDarkly, Unleash, Flagsmith - switch vendors and rewrite everything.

OpenFeature: one API, any backend. CNCF standard.
